title: DA-review protocol extension for calendar-driven proposals
source: [backlog_reviews/20260526-backlog-full-review_v1.json](backlog_reviews/20260526-backlog-full-review_v1.json) objection 6 (medium); promoted out of B0017 because expanding DA-review scope is a methodology change, not a feature.
why: The current LLM lookahead linter does NOT catch event-calendar references — no calendar dates appear in proposal text, but implicit references to public schedules (Fed meetings, BLS releases) are still lookahead-shaped. Expanding the DA protocol to include a `calendar_leakage_check` is a load-bearing methodology change that deserves its own meta-decision with steel-man and objections, not a sentence buried inside a feature entry.
value_rationale: M (downgraded from H in 2026-05-27 recalibration — gates B0017 which is itself B0000-deferred; not currently load-bearing on any active work-stream)
scope: add a new review category to [.claude/agents/phase5-devils-advocate.md](.claude/agents/phase5-devils-advocate.md) that explicitly checks calendar-driven proposals for (a) implicit schedule references in the hypothesis text, (b) post-publication-but-pre-pricing windows being exploited as features rather than declared lookahead, (c) whether the probe matrix in B0008 covers a calendar-null cell. Document the new category in [phase5/AUDIT_REVIEW.md](phase5/AUDIT_REVIEW.md).
